title: tests: fix diff cmd test on macOS HFS+, fixes #8860
body: If we touch too quickly after file creation, it would not noticably update the timestamp.

files_modified.patch:
@@ -53,6 +53,7 @@ def test_basic_functionality(archivers, request):
     create_regular_file(archiver.input_path, "file_replaced", contents=b"0" * 4096)
     os.unlink("input/file_removed")
     os.unlink("input/file_removed2")
+    time.sleep(1)  # macOS HFS+ has a 1s timestamp granularity
     Path("input/file_touched").touch()
     os.rmdir("input/dir_replaced_with_file")
     create_regular_file(archiver.input_path, "dir_replaced_with_file", size=8192)

steps.operation: Add a delay before touching a file to ensure its mtime changes on filesystems with coarse timestamp granularity
steps.reason: On macOS HFS+ the timestamp granularity is 1 second, so touching a file immediately after creation may not update the modification time, causing the diff command test to be flaky; sleeping for 1 second guarantees a detectable mtime change.
steps.side_effects: Slightly increases the runtime of this test (and any suite that includes it) by ~1s; reduces flakiness specifically on macOS/HFS+ and potentially other filesystems with 1s timestamp resolution.

step_plan.summary: Fixes a flaky diff command test on macOS HFS+ by sleeping 1 second before touching a file so the file’s mtime change is reliably detected.
